rulings.Case Title: PAUL JAMES DOUGLASS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 2, DEFENDANT GARDNER DENVER, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BASED ON THE DECLARATIONS OF SANDLER AND SILLOWAY. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS EVIDENCE OF TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON THE DECLARATIONS OF PLAINTIFF, AYE AND BURCH. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT THE DANGERS OF ASBESTOS WERE KNOWN OR SHOULD HAVE BEEN KNOWN TO THE CLASS OF WORKERS PERFORMING THE TASKS PLAINTIFF WAS PERFORMING. THE SILLOWAY DECLARATION IS SILENT ON WHAT THE NAVY KNEW OF THE DANGERS OF ASBESTOS WHEN PURCHASING DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED FOR IT IS BASED ON CAUSATION. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL JAMES DOUGLASS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 2, DEFENDANT CRANE CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SURE BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC. et al VS. TCC STEEL, A KOREAN COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 3, DEFENDANTS BONG-RAK SOHN, YOL-HO SOHN, YONG BIN, YONG BIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY AND DEFENDANTS' DECLARATIONS. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT OR ESTABLISHES A BASIS TO IMPOSE INDIVIDUAL LIABILITY OF DEFENDANTS FOR A BUSINESS TRANSACTION BETWEEN TWO CORPORATIONS. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLACE IN EVIDENCE THE 2000 REPORT AND CHOY'S DECLARATION LACKS ANY FOUNDATIONAL FACTS THAT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR CHOY'S STATEMENTS IN PARAGRAPH 23. DEFENDANT HAS EXPLAINED WHY MR. YOL HO SOHN DID NOT APPEAR AT HIS DEPOSITION. GIVEN THE TESTIMONY OF THE OTHER DEPOSED CORPORATE OFFICERS, THE COURT FAILS TO SEE ANY BASIS FOR BELIEVING THAT THIS WITNESS, WHO IS 86 AND IN POOR HEALTH, IS CAPABLE OF PROVIDING ANY EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GERARD MOONEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 4, DEFENDANT ROBERT E MCKEE INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT DID NOT OBTAIN RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT IS UNABLE TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS BY DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES. THE MOONEY AND AY DECLARATIONS ALSO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: GERARD MOONEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 4, DEFENDANT ROBERTSHAW CONTROLS COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS NOT EXCLUDED UNDER D'AMICO. CONSTRUING THE DECLARATION IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF AS THE COURT MUST DO, THE REASONABLE INFERENCE IS THAT DEFENDANT'S WORK DISTURBED ASBESTOS IN THE CEILING. THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT SEE IT HAPPEN GOES TO THE PERSUASIVENESS OF THE EVIDENCE NOT ITS ADMISSIBILITY. AY HAS SUFFICIENT EXPERTISE TO OFFER THE OPINION STATED. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: GERARD MOONEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 4, DEFENDANT ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE MATERIALS SUPPLIED BY ALTA TO WHICH PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS AS DESCRIBED IN PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION. DEFENDANT CANNOT RELY ON KAISER'S RESPONSES TO MEET ITS BURDEN. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATIONS ALSO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY WALTHER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 5, DEFENDANT D.W. NICHOLSON CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATIONS CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BECAUSE THE REASONABLE INFERENCE FROM THE DECLARATIONS IS THAT PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS THAT CAME FROM HIS FATHER'S WORK FOR DEFENDANT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY WALTHER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 5, DEFENDANT LAMONS METAL GASKET COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. IN MOVING FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, DEFENDANT RELIES ON THE TESTIMONY OF LEVER IN OTHER CASES, WHICH IS NOT ADMISSIBLE AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND ON THE OWEN DECLARATION WHICH FAILS TO ATTACH TWO EXHIBITS UPON WHICH OWEN'S TESTIMONY IS BASED. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS L 1, N 4-8 ARE SUSTAINED; THE REMAINDER ARE OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MULLEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 8, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(d) THAT REQUIRES REFERENCE TO THE EXHIBIT, TITLE, PAGE AND LINE NUMBERS. IT IS UNREASONABLE TO EXPECT THE COURT TO REVIEW VOLUMINOUS RECORDS TO CONFIRM THAT AN ASSERTED FACT IS TRUE. SEE DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT 8, 9, 10, 13, 19, 20, 23, 25, 26, 31, 32. =(302/PJM/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RESTAURANTS INTERNATIONAL, INC. A CORPORATION et al VS. NANCY MAR et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 12, DEFENDANT NANCY MAR MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. IT IS NOT APPARENT WHY PLAINTIFF SEEKS THIS FORM OF RELIEF OR WHY SUCH RELIEF IS A NECESSARY PART OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR GOODWIN'S ALLEGED FRAUDULENT TRANSFER. GOODWIN IS NOT A PARTY TO THIS CASE AND IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS A DETERMINATION OF PLAINTIFF'S RIGHTS AGAINST GOODWIN THEN PLAINTIFF SHOULD MAKE THE CLAIM AGAINST GOODWIN'S ESTATE. =(302/PJM/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HARRY WOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Joinder) In Demurrer To Amended Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JULY 18, 2008, LINE 14, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST RONALD DONG, JEANETTE CORDERO (Joinder) In Demurrer To Amended Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ALL PARTIES ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THE TENANTS WERE INDISPENSABLE PARTIES AND WERE NOT NAMED AS PARTIES WITHIN 90 DAYS AS REQUESTED BY CCP SECTION 1094.6. THE FACT THAT THE PETITION WAS SENT TO THE TENANTS DOES NOT CHANGE THE RESULT. =(302/PJM/AY)
